title: Deliciously Crunchy Croatian Meat Wrap
description: A traditional Croatian dish featuring a yeasty and crunchy corn husk wrap filled with rest meat and spices, perfect for curing hangovers. This dish is a hearty and flavorful meal that combines the warmth of cinnamon with the comfort of slow cooked meat.

ingredients.name: ground beef
ingredients.notes: rest meat, such as leftover roast beef or beef stew, can be used as a substitute

ingredients.name: paprika
ingredients.notes: smoked or sweet paprika can be used

ingredients.name: corn husks
ingredients.notes: fresh or dried corn husks can be used

instructions: In a large pan, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium heat and cook the diced onion until it is translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, until fragrant.
Add the ground beef and cook until it is browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the broken cinnamon stick, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Reduce heat to low and simmer the mixture for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
While the meat mixture is cooking, prepare the corn husks by soaking them in water for 30 minutes.
Once the corn husks are pliable, remove them from the water and fill each husk with about 1/2 cup of the meat mixture.
Fold the sides of the corn husk over the filling and roll the wrap up tightly, repeating with the remaining husks and filling.
Place the wraps on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake in a preheated oven at 375 degrees for 20 minutes, or until the corn husks are crispy and golden brown.
Remove the wraps from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the wraps hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F.
